honesty_note: Curated published survey figures, paraphrased for citation — not an independent primary dataset. All citation_types are secondary (no original primary research conducted).

facts.claim: While the vast majority of enterprises deploy artificial intelligence within at least one core corporate function, a minor fraction of organizations achieve substantial bottom-line efficiency increases.
facts.source: McKinsey, The State of AI in 2025
facts.usage_note: Use this fact as context to challenge claims that tool deployment automatically equates to enterprise value realization.

facts.claim: Nearly one-third of generative AI initiatives are discontinued following the initial trial phase due to obstacles surrounding data cleanliness, unpredicted deployment costs, and unproven business results.
facts.usage_note: Apply this metric to discount financial forecasts that assume all exploratory pilots successfully scale to full operational maturity.

facts.claim: Chief Marketing Officers dedicate a noticeable percentage of their operational funding to intelligence tools, despite widespread internal recognition that existing team structures lack the maturity to optimize the technology.
facts.usage_note: Utilize this baseline range to determine if a target organization's project spending aligns with standard industry capital allocation strategies.

facts.claim: A small segment of highly mature corporate marketing teams secure expanded baseline budgets and assign significantly larger financial allocations to automated tools.
facts.usage_note: Treat this figure as an absolute ceiling for marketing budget allocation, applicable only to mature digital operations.

facts.claim: AI-powered content drafting systems show the highest isolated marketing ROI among measured applications.
facts.usage_note: Deploy this granular multiplier to validate functional claims for content generation rather than accepting broad, unsegmented return assumptions.

facts.claim: Personalization engines yield moderate-to-strong ROI among isolated marketing AI applications.
facts.usage_note: Deploy this granular multiplier to validate functional claims for personalization rather than accepting broad, unsegmented return assumptions.

facts.claim: AI audience targeting and research tools deliver measurable but lower ROI compared to content generation applications.
facts.usage_note: Deploy this granular multiplier to validate functional claims for audience targeting rather than accepting broad, unsegmented return assumptions.

facts.claim: Ad copy optimization solutions show the lowest ROI among the measured isolated marketing AI applications.
facts.usage_note: Deploy this granular multiplier to validate functional claims for ad copy optimization rather than accepting broad, unsegmented return assumptions.

facts.claim: Deeply embedding automated features directly into marketing workflows yields noticeable gains in user acquisition efficiency, customer response rates, and absolute return performance.
facts.usage_note: Apply these optimal benchmarks only when a company presents verified plans for deep business workflow transformations.

facts.claim: Defensible long-term outcomes for mature, integrated marketing architectures center around moderate return lifts or clear application-specific multiples.
facts.usage_note: Establish this range as the realistic baseline for core financial models validating long-term campaign returns.

facts.claim: The time required to fully break even on investments differs significantly depending on whether a company chooses basic cloud subscriptions or implements highly customized software configurations.
facts.usage_note: Flag any business plan claiming a sub-six-month capital recovery for deeply customized integrations as overly optimistic.
